steps.title: Identify the subject and its number
steps.content: The subject is The Proto-Nilotic language — singular. The long appositive phrase "common ancestor of fifty-five African languages with similar linguistic properties" modifies the subject but does not change its singular number.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'is,': A singular present-tense verb that correctly agrees with the singular subject "The Proto-Nilotic language." ✓
  • Choice B — 'are,': A plural verb; it violates subject-verb agreement with the singular subject. ✗
  • Choice C — 'have been,': A plural verb (present perfect); it violates subject-verb agreement with the singular subject. ✗
  • Choice D — 'were,': A plural past-tense verb; it violates subject-verb agreement with the singular subject. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
